LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ANDREW DIOLI'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS, DEFENDANT ANDREW DIOLI'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS IS DENIED. WHILE MR. DIOLI SAYS THAT 98 ASHLAND DRIVE IS NOT HIS RESIDENCE OR PLACE OF WORK AND HIS REPLY MEMORANDUM STATES THAT HE IS AMENABLE TO SERVICE OF PROCESS, NEITHER HE NOR HIS ATTORNEY PROVIDE ANY MEANS BY WHICH HE CAN BE SERVED. MR. DIOLI'S ATTITUDE TOWARD SERVICE OR PROCESS, COUPLED WITH THE INFORMATION IN MR. RAICHELSON'S DECLARATION, STRONGLY SUGGESTS THAT HE IS TRYING TO EVADE SERVICE AND THAT HIS DECLARATION IS NOT CREDIBLE. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT WHICH MAY BE E-MAILED TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-14-2016 Continued To Jul-19-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Mar-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MICHAEL RAICHELSON, ESQ. of Persolve, LLC, Tel No. 818-534-3105 appearing telephonically for the Plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this matter.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT ANDREW DIOLI'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS, Defendant Andrew Dioli's motion to quash service of summons is denied. While Mr. Dioli says that 98 Ashland Drive is not his residence or place of work and his reply memorandum states that he is amenable to service of process, neither he nor his attorney provide any means by which he can be served. Mr. Dioli's attitude toward service or process, coupled with the information in Mr. Raichelson's declaration, strongly suggests that he is trying to evade service and that his declaration is not credible. Plaintiff's counsel shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court which may be e-mailed to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment Pursuant To Ccp §473(D); And To Quash Service Of Summons (Ccp §418.10)
Off Calendar Apr-02-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT ANDREW DIOLI'S Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment Pursuant To CCP Secs. 167; 473(D); And To Quash Service Of Summons (CCP Secs. 167; 418.10), the motion to vacate the default and default judgment and motion to quash service is granted. The evidence discloses that plaintiff attempted to effectuate service at the wrong address. The default and default judgment are void. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H.
